title: find_header_end: LF-only blank line missed when CRLF is present later
description:
TRIGGER: Malformed raw message containing 

 (bare LF blank line) in the headers section before a later 

 (CRLF blank line). Example: b'X: a

Y: b

Body'. BAD BEHAVIOR: find_header_end searches for 

 first; it finds the CRLF separator at the later position. The 

 at position 5 is ignored. Everything between position 0 and the CRLF separator — including what is semantically the message body — is treated as the header section and parsed for header fields. Body lines that happen to contain ':' will be incorrectly extracted as headers, causing false positive matches in header/address/exists tests. Well-formed messages are unaffected. The fix is to take the minimum of the two separator positions.
